$comment: Claude Code settings for the Linus repo. PostToolUse hooks run ruff format/isort-via-ruff/ruff check on Python file edits at line length 120, and prettier --write on Markdown file edits using the .prettierrc.json config (printWidth 120, proseWrap always — matches the Python convention). .prettierignore excludes repos/, modules/, context/, and lockfiles. mdlint is deliberately NOT in the hook chain — mdlint 0.3.15's `check` subcommand silently auto-fixes some rules (e.g. MD004 list marker style) even without --fix, which can corrupt prose containing characters that look like list markers. Use mdlint manually with confirmation. Permissions allowlist is path-scoped where the command takes a file argument (PDF tools, mkdir, git add, prettier) so we cannot accidentally auto-run on paths outside the Linus tree. See CLAUDE.md (Tool Use Policy) and BRANCHING.md (allowed branch types) for the source-of-truth policy this list enforces.

hooks.command: if [[ "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" == *.py ]]; then ruff format --line-length 120 "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" 2>&1 || true; fi
if [[ "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" == *.py ]]; then ruff check --select I --fix "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" 2>&1 || true; fi
if [[ "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" == *.py ]]; then ruff check "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" 2>&1 || true; fi
